title: How to Create a Task Plan
author: Neela Thiagarajan

overview.business_outcome: Planners understand what a Task Plan is, when to use it, and why it must exist before any campaign or work order can be created
overview.data_triggers: Task Plan must exist before Standard Work Order can link to it · Checklist items and billing codes defined once and inherited by all work orders
overview.content:
A Task Plan in the EAM system defines HOW maintenance work is performed. It replaces the legacy Job Plan and serves as the foundational work template for generating work orders.

A Task Plan specifies the standard, repeatable instructions that appear on every work order:
- Checklist items (inspection steps or measurement points)
- Billing relief codes (valid completion outcomes for automated work order closure)
- Reference documents (linked by name/code to content on handheld devices)
- Follow-up rules (conditions that trigger additional work orders)

Task Plans are REUSABLE. One Task Plan can be referenced by multiple Standard Work Orders and PM Schedules. Used by both WGC (Corrective Maintenance) and PMC (Preventive Maintenance) campaigns.

prerequisites.business_outcome: Task Plan creation succeeds on first attempt — no rework from missing Engineering inputs or incorrect document references
prerequisites.data_triggers: Document names must match exactly what exists on handheld devices · Engineering is source of truth for all Task Plan content
prerequisites.content:
Before creating a Task Plan you must have:

1. Access to the EAM system with Task Plan creation permissions
2. Work instructions and checklist items from Engineering — what technicians will inspect or perform, whether inspection-based or measurement-based
3. Billing relief codes from Engineering — at least two reference numbers (entered manually in current phase)
4. Document name/code from Engineering — must match EXACTLY what exists on handheld devices
5. Sub Work Type value if required — confirm with Process SME (pending confirmation — leave blank until confirmed)
6. Next available Task Plan number — check list view first, numbers are sequential

steps.business_outcome: Task Plan created with all required components — checklist, billing codes, document reference — ready to be linked by Standard Work Orders and PM Schedules
steps.process_stage: Task Plan Creation → Link to Standard Work Order or PM Schedule
steps.data_triggers: Work Order Type = Campaign must be set before Save · Active Checklist checkbox must remain unchecked · Required Entry must be Warning not Yes · Follow-up Task Plan must be linked on Sequence 10
steps.content:
Steps to Create a Task Plan:

STEP 1 — Header Details:
1. Navigate: Work > Work Order Planning > Task Plans
2. Click New Record icon
3. Enter unique Task Plan number (sequential — check list view first)
4. Enter Description — clear and searchable
5. Set Work Order Type = Campaign (CRITICAL before Save)

STEP 2 — Billing Relief Codes (Billing tab):
1. Navigate to Billing tab
2. Add at least two billing relief codes from Engineering — enter reference numbers manually

STEP 3 — Checklist (Checklist tab):
1. Navigate to Checklist tab
2. Add items with sequence numbers 10, 20, 30 (increment by 10)
3. Sequence 10 = inspection/primary, Sequence 20 = measurement (wear chart)
4. For wear chart: check Enable Nonconformities; set type = Measurement
5. Follow-up Task Plan: link on Sequence 10 ONLY
6. Do NOT check Active Checklist checkbox
7. Set Required Entry = Warning (not Yes)

STEP 4 — Documents (More tab > Documents):
1. Navigate More tab > Documents
2. Add Document — enter name/code exactly as Engineering provided
3. Check Copy to Work Order on PRIMARY document only

STEP 5 — Save:
Enter Sub Work Type if required and confirmed, then click Save

VERIFY: Task Plan visible in list, all tabs populated

rules.business_outcome: Task Plan errors prevented at creation time — no rework from incorrect type, checklist configuration, or document setup failures
rules.process_stage: Task Plan Creation → Link to Standard Work Order or PM Schedule
rules.data_triggers: Work Order Type = Campaign required for lookup visibility · Active Checklist controls checklist display · Required Entry = Yes blocks automated closure · Follow-up Task Plan on Sequence 10 triggers follow-up WO
rules.content:
Critical Rules — Task Plan:

WORK ORDER TYPE MUST BE CAMPAIGN
Must be set before Save. If wrong, Task Plan won't appear in Standard Work Order Activities tab lookup.

DO NOT CHECK ACTIVE CHECKLIST
If Active Checklist checkbox is checked, checklist items will NOT appear on generated work orders.

REQUIRED ENTRY = WARNING NOT YES
If set to Yes on any checklist item, Mobile field app blocks work order closure. Automated closure will fail.

FOLLOW-UP TASK PLAN ON SEQUENCE 10 ONLY
The follow-up Task Plan field on Sequence 20 is NOT honored by the system.

SEQUENCE NUMBERS IN INCREMENTS OF 10
Use 10, 20, 30 not 1, 2, 3. Allows future insertions without renumbering.

ONE DOCUMENT WITH COPY TO WORK ORDER
Multiple checked documents cause unpredictable behavior on handhelds.

DOCUMENTS ARE REFERENCES NOT UPLOADS
File lives on handheld devices. Name must match exactly.

DO NOT DUPLICATE TASK PLANS
Check if a suitable Task Plan already exists before creating a new one.

troubleshoot.business_outcome: Task Plan configuration errors resolved without work order regeneration or data corruption
troubleshoot.process_stage: Task Plan Creation → Work Order Generation → Field Execution
troubleshoot.data_triggers: Work Order Type = Campaign required for lookup · Active Checklist controls display · Required Entry = Yes blocks closure · Follow-up Task Plan on Sequence 10 triggers follow-up WO
troubleshoot.content:
Task Plan Troubleshooting:

Problem: Task Plan not visible in Standard Work Order Activities tab lookup
Cause: Work Order Type not set to Campaign, or wrong organization.
Fix: Verify Work Order Type = Campaign. Search by partial number. Confirm correct organization.

Problem: Checklist items not appearing on generated work orders
Cause: Active Checklist checkbox is checked on the Task Plan.
Fix: Open Task Plan — confirm Active Checklist is NOT checked. Verify Task Plan is linked on SWO Activities tab.

Problem: Work orders not closing automatically after field submission
Cause: Required Entry is set to Yes on one or more checklist items.
Fix: Open Task Plan > Checklist — change Required Entry from Yes to Warning on all items.

Problem: Follow-up work order not created after inspection
Cause: Follow-up Task Plan linked on Sequence 20 instead of Sequence 10.
Fix: Open Task Plan > Checklist — select Sequence 10 and link follow-up Task Plan there.

Problem: Primary document missing from field user's work order on handheld
Cause: Copy to Work Order not checked, or document name doesn't match handheld.
Fix: Confirm Copy to Work Order checked on primary document only. Confirm document name matches Engineering confirmation.

glossary.business_outcome: Users understand key Task Plan terms before creating or troubleshooting
glossary.data_triggers: Term definitions — reference only, no system actions triggered
glossary.content:
Task Plan Glossary:

Task Plan: Work instruction template replacing legacy Job Plan. Reusable across multiple work orders and campaigns.

Billing Relief Code: Code defining valid completion outcome. When technician enters matching code, system can auto-close the work order.

Active Checklist (checkbox): If checked, checklist items DO NOT appear on work orders. Must remain unchecked.

Required Entry: Must be set to Warning not Yes. If Yes, automated work order closure is blocked.

Sequence Number: Order number for checklist items (10, 20, 30). Increments of 10 allow future insertions.

Follow-up Task Plan: Linked on Sequence 10 only. Triggers creation of additional work order when activated.

Copy to Work Order: Designates primary document for mobile field application. Only one document should have this checked.

Document Reference: Link to document name/code on handheld devices. EAM system does not store the file itself.

Nonconformity: Mechanism for measurement-based checklist items (wear chart). Captures recorded values for threshold evaluation.
